_attribution.basis: These entries record factual framework-to-FAIR-CAM mapping RELATIONSHIPS referenced from the FAIR Institute's published NIST CSF 1.1 and CIS 8.0 -> FAIR-CAM 1.0 crosswalks, and independently expressed as structured data. Framework subcategory text is published by NIST (NIST CSF; US-government public domain) and the Center for Internet Security (CIS Controls). The mapping relationships are recorded as facts; no copyright or license claim is made or implied over them. Attribution to the FAIR Institute is provided as the reference source. RiskFlow-added extension functions (see per-entry citation.riskflow_extension) are RiskFlow methodology decisions, not part of the FAIR Institute source documents. See data/seed_framework_crosswalk.NOTICE.md.
_attribution.extensions: 5 CIS safeguard entries carry an additional RiskFlow-added FAIR-CAM function in 'riskflow_extension_functions' (per-entry rationale in that entry's citation.riskflow_extension). These are RiskFlow methodology decisions, NOT part of the FAIR Institute source documents; 'fair_cam_functions' remains a faithful transcription of the source X-marks.

entries.title: Physical devices and systems within the organization are inventoried
Software platforms and applications within the organization are inventoried
Organizational communication and data flows are mapped
External information systems are catalogued
Resources (e.g., hardware, devices, data, time, personnel, and software) are prioritized based on their classification, criticality, and business value

Suppliers and third party partners of information systems, components, and services are identified, prioritized, and assessed using a cyber supply chain risk assessment process

Protection processes are improved

citation.riskflow_extension: RiskFlow adds lec_prev_avoidance to this safeguard's FAIR-CAM functions (the FAIR-Institute crosswalk maps 4.8 -> lec_prev_resistance only). Rationale: uninstalling/disabling unnecessary services removes reachable service endpoints = attack-surface removal, which per RiskFlow rubric §4-I1 removes threat-contact vectors (reduces TEF/contact) → LEC Avoidance, distinct from Example-3 Resistance (which reduces exploitability AFTER contact). Methodology decision, #437 rollout T2. Collateral verified: the only seed entries carrying CIS 4.8 are Hardened OS/Services (HAOS) and Hardened SaaS Application (HASS), both T2 avoidance adds that genuinely deliver surface-removal avoidance — no other entry gains a channel from this extension.

citation.riskflow_extension: RiskFlow adds lec_prev_resistance to this safeguard's FAIR-CAM functions (the FAIR-Institute crosswalk maps 7.3 -> vmc_corr_implementation only). Rationale: RiskFlow rubric §4-I1 Ex3 assigns patching an OS/app CVE to LEC Resistance; this deliberately supersedes the FAIR-Institute crosswalk's patch -> VMC-Correction reading for channel assignment. Methodology decision, #437 rollout T1 (grounds Patch Management's genuine home instead of the reverse-selected CIS 4.6 graft).

citation.riskflow_extension: RiskFlow adds lec_prev_resistance to this safeguard's FAIR-CAM functions (the FAIR-Institute crosswalk maps 14.2 -> DSC Prevention only). Rationale: a workforce member trained to recognize social-engineering lures reduces the probability the human-vector threat action (clicking a phishing link, opening a weaponized attachment, divulging credentials) succeeds — lowering the Vulnerability of the human-mediated attack path (rubric §4-I1 Example-3 analog: reduces the probability a threat action succeeds → LEC Resistance). This is distinct from the earlier PEPL claim-drop, which rejected the CONTRIVED tags PR.AC-1/PR.IP-3/CIS 16.10 (access-control/change-control/app-design) that an awareness posture does not carry; CIS 14.2 (social-engineering-recognition training) IS a genuine Security-Conscious-Personnel home. Methodology decision, #437 rollout T2. Collateral verified: the only seed entries carrying CIS 14.2 are Security Conscious Personnel (PEPL, this T2 add) and Security Awareness Training (SAT); SAT does NOT claim lec_prev_resistance, so this extension adds a scoring channel to no other entry.

Establish and Maintain a Secure Application Development Process

citation.riskflow_extension: RiskFlow adds lec_prev_resistance to this safeguard's FAIR-CAM functions (the FAIR-Institute crosswalk maps 16.1 -> DSC/VMC only). Rationale: a secure application-development process (input validation, parameterized queries, output encoding, vetted APIs) reduces the exploitable-defect density of the shipped software application, lowering the probability an exploit attempt against the application succeeds. Per rubric §4-I1 Example-3 the application is the ASSET and its code-level defects are its own susceptibility, so reducing that susceptibility is LEC Resistance (reduces Vulnerability), not VMC-Correction. Methodology decision, #437 rollout T2. Collateral verified: seed entries carrying CIS 16.1 are Secure Coding Practices (SCP, this T2 add), Secured Software (SSW — already claims lec_prev_resistance grounded by its existing PR.PT-3, so redundant, no change), and DAST/SAST (testing controls that do NOT claim lec_prev_resistance) — this extension adds a scoring channel to no other entry.
